title: Explaining previous work
steps: I worked.
I worked on reports.
I worked on weekly reports.
I worked on weekly performance reports.
I worked on weekly performance reports for multiple clients.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Describing responsibilities
steps: I was responsible.
I was responsible for checking data.
I was responsible for checking data every day.
I was responsible for checking data and updating the team.
I was responsible for checking data and updating the team before the deadline.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Introducing your background
steps: I have experience.
I have experience in customer support.
I have experience in customer support and communication.
I have experience in customer support and communication with clients.
I have experience in customer support and communication with clients through chat and email.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Explaining a strength
steps: I am organized.
I am organized with my tasks.
I am organized with my tasks and deadlines.
I am organized with my tasks and deadlines, especially when work is busy.
I am organized with my tasks and deadlines, especially when work is busy and priorities change.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Asking for repetition
steps: Please repeat that.
Could you please repeat that?
Could you please repeat the last part?
Could you please repeat the last part more slowly?
Could you please repeat the last part more slowly so I can answer correctly?
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Checking understanding
steps: I understood the point.
I think I understood the point.
I think I understood the main point.
I think I understood the main point, but I want to confirm one detail.
I think I understood the main point, but I want to confirm one detail before I answer.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Updating progress
steps: I checked it.
I checked the file.
I checked the file and found an issue.
I checked the file and found an issue that needs review.
I checked the file and found an issue that needs review before we continue.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Explaining a delay
steps: I need time.
I need a little more time.
I need a little more time to check this.
I need a little more time to check this properly.
I need a little more time to check this properly and avoid giving the wrong answer.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Showing empathy
steps: I understand.
I understand your concern.
I understand your concern and I’m checking this.
I understand your concern and I’m checking this for you.
I understand your concern and I’m checking this for you as carefully as possible.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Offering help
steps: I can help.
I can help you.
I can help you with that.
I can help you with that and guide you through the next step.
I can help you with that and guide you through the next step if you can share a few details.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Explaining confidence
steps: I can do this.
I can do this task.
I can do this task with guidance.
I can do this task with guidance and regular practice.
I can do this task with guidance and regular practice, and I am willing to learn.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Learning new tools
steps: I can learn.
I can learn new tools.
I can learn new tools quickly.
I can learn new tools quickly if I get clear instructions.
I can learn new tools quickly if I get clear instructions and enough practice.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Present perfect practice
steps: I have worked.
I have worked on reports.
I have worked on reports before.
I have worked on reports before in my previous role.
I have worked on reports before in my previous role and I am comfortable with similar tasks.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Past simple practice
steps: I completed it.
I completed the task.
I completed the task yesterday.
I completed the task yesterday and shared the update.
I completed the task yesterday and shared the update with the team.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Starting a conversation
steps: Good morning.
Good morning, how are you?
Good morning, how are you today?
Good morning, I hope you’re doing well today.
Good morning, I hope you’re doing well today and thank you for speaking with me.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Answering why this role 16
steps: I am interested.
I am interested in this role.
I am interested in this role because it matches my skills.
I am interested in this role because it matches my skills and goals.
I am interested in this role because it matches my skills and gives me room to grow.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Discussing weakness 17
steps: I am improving.
I am improving my English.
I am improving my English speaking skills.
I am improving my English speaking skills through daily practice.
I am improving my English speaking skills through daily practice and structured interview preparation.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Describing tools 18
steps: I used tools.
I used online tools.
I used online tools for daily work.
I used online tools for daily work, reporting, and communication.
I used online tools for daily work, reporting, and communication with team members.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Explaining teamwork 19
steps: I worked with a team.
I worked with a small team.
I worked with a small team on daily tasks.
I worked with a small team on daily tasks and shared updates regularly.
I worked with a small team on daily tasks and shared updates regularly to keep everyone aligned.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Clarifying deadline 20
steps: When is it due?
Could you tell me when it is due?
Could you tell me when this task is due?
Could you tell me when this task is due so I can plan properly?
Could you tell me when this task is due so I can plan properly and prioritize the work?
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Requesting details 21
steps: Please share details.
Could you please share the details?
Could you please share the details of the task?
Could you please share the details of the task and the expected outcome?
Could you please share the details of the task and the expected outcome so I can complete it correctly?
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Requesting review 22
steps: Please review this.
Could you please review this?
Could you please review this when you have time?
Could you please review this when you have time and let me know if changes are needed?
Could you please review this when you have time and let me know if any changes are needed before submission?
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Completed task 23
steps: It is done.
The task is done.
The task is done and updated.
The task is done and updated in the file.
The task is done and updated in the file for your review.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Avoiding guessing 24
steps: I am not sure.
I am not fully sure.
I am not fully sure, so I will check.
I am not fully sure, so I will check before answering.
I am not fully sure, so I will check before answering to avoid giving incorrect information.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Acknowledging issue 25
steps: I see the issue.
I see the issue you mentioned.
I see the issue you mentioned and I’m checking it.
I see the issue you mentioned and I’m checking the details now.
I see the issue you mentioned and I’m checking the details now so I can guide you correctly.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: Handling pressure 26
steps: I stay calm.
I try to stay calm.
I try to stay calm under pressure.
I try to stay calm under pressure and focus on the next step.
I try to stay calm under pressure and focus on the next step instead of rushing.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

title: For vs since 27
steps: I have worked here.
I have worked here for one year.
I have worked here since last year.
I have worked here for one year and learned many things.
I have worked here for one year and learned many things from my team.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

steps: Nice to meet you.
It’s nice to meet you.
It’s nice to meet you and thank you for your time.
It’s nice to meet you and thank you for taking the time to speak with me.
It’s nice to meet you and thank you for taking the time to speak with me today.
tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.
